# Contributing

Thanks for your interest in contributing! This document outlines how to get involved.

## Getting Started

1. Fork the repository and clone your fork locally.
2. Install dependencies per the instructions in the README.
3. Create a new branch for your work: `git checkout -b my-feature`.

## Making Changes

- Keep changes focused and scoped to a single concern per pull request.
- Follow the existing code style and conventions.
- Add or update tests for any behavior you change.
- Update documentation when relevant.

## Commit Messages

Write clear, descriptive commit messages. A short summary line followed by a longer explanation when needed is ideal.

## Pull Requests

1. Push your branch to your fork and open a pull request against `main`.
2. Describe what the change does and why it is needed.
3. Link any related issues.
4. Ensure CI checks pass before requesting review.

## Reporting Issues

When opening an issue, please include:

- A clear description of the problem.
- Steps to reproduce.
- Expected vs. actual behavior.
- Relevant environment details (OS, version, etc.).

## Code of Conduct

Be respectful and constructive. Assume good intent and engage with others professionally.

## Questions

If you're unsure about anything, open a discussion or draft PR — we're happy to help.
